name: VCR.py
description: Python library that records HTTP interactions on first run and replays them in subsequent tests, making suites deterministic and fast.

seo.title: VCR.py — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: VCR.py: Python library that records HTTP interactions on first run and replays them in subsequent tests, making suites deterministic and fast.

when_to_use: Record real HTTP interactions as cassettes for Python tests
Deterministic test runs replaying recorded API responses
Avoid flaky tests caused by live third-party API changes
Speed up Python tests by eliminating network latency

pros: Records real HTTP once, replays forever
Cassettes commit to git for reproducibility
cons: Stale cassettes silently hide API changes
Secrets leak into cassettes without filtering
Re-recording cassettes is tedious at scale
review.verdict: A powerful pattern for third-party API tests, but stale cassettes cause more pain than most teams expect.
review.best_for: Python projects with many third-party HTTP integrations needing fast test runs
review.not_for: Fast-moving external APIs where cassette drift hides real regressions
